rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN B. NELSON VS. BRENDAN M MCGRATH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication By Defendant Cross-Complainant Brendan M Mcgrath Dba Morern Art Construction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT BRENDAN MCGRATH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is DENIED. There are multiple triable issues of fact in this action. See opposition. Plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES WANNINGER ET AL VS. 195-205 TAMAL VISTA BLVD LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 195-205 Tamal Vista Blvd, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike A Portion Of First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT 195-205 TAMAL VISTA BLVD LLC's NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE A PORTION OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and MOOT in part. Motion is granted to the extent prayer for damages includes attorney's fees incurred in the first UD action. That matter has already been finally resolved in the UD action. Motion is moot to the extent the prayer for damages includes attorney's fees incurred in the second and third UD actions as claims pertaining to those actions have been stricken. See Ruling on Anti-SLAPP. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES WANNINGER ET AL VS. 195-205 TAMAL VISTA BLVD LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT 195-205 TAMAL VISTA BLVD LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED as requested. Portions of the complaint outlined in the notice of the motion 2:12-13 are stricken as Plaintiff Lillian Wanniger failed to carry her burden under the second prong of ANTI-SLAPP. Portions the complained outlined in the Notice of Motion 2:24-28 are stricken as Plaintiff failed to carry his burden on the second prong of the ANTI-SLAPP on all elements of the malicious prosecution claim in the second and third unlawful detainer actions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES WANNINGER ET AL VS. 195-205 TAMAL VISTA BLVD LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Trivon Simmons-Kraffts Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike The First Amended Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT TRIVON SIMMONS-KRAFFT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 425.16 is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Strike is granted as to any references to the second and third UD action in the malicious prosecution cause of action. Motion is DENIED as to the causes of action for retaliation. Plaintiff met his burden under the second prong of the ANTI-SLAPP. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
